scenarios.opening_query: The state took my daycare license over an incident that was completely misreported.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Professional Licensing || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: The same misreport put ME on the child-abuse central registry — challenging that founded disposition is separate from the license case and controls it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The same report put me on the child-abuse registry — challenging that disposition is its own case and controls the license one.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a registry finding underlies the license action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No registry finding — it's a straight licensing-division suspension with a hearing date.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No registry finding — just the licensing suspension and a hearing date.
scenarios.vetting_note: Registry-vs-license proceedings are distinct systems users conflate.

scenarios.opening_query: My food cart was shut down by an inspector who barely looked at anything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal) || General Litigation > Civil Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: He shut down all four Somali-owned carts on our block the same day and none of the others — we've compared write-ups and the pattern is unmistakable.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He shut all four Somali-owned carts the same day and nobody else — we compared write-ups and the pattern's unmistakable.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether enforcement targeted a protected group
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Everyone on the block got hit equally — my appeal is that the code section he cited doesn't even apply to carts.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Everyone got hit equally — my appeal is that his code section doesn't apply to carts.

scenarios.opening_query: My veteran husband's claim has been 'pending' so long that we've given up on the normal channels.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Military/Veterans || Administrative Law > PERS
scenarios.hidden_fact: The stuck claim isn't the VA at all — it's PERS refusing to credit his military service years toward his state-trooper pension, which their own rules allow.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's not the VA — it's PERS refusing to credit his military years toward his trooper pension, which their rules allow.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which retirement system's decision is stuck
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's his VA disability-rating increase — three years pending, two 'lost' C&P exams.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's his VA rating increase — three years pending and two 'lost' exams.
scenarios.vetting_note: Military-service-credit disputes live in PERS, not the VA; agency identity routes.

scenarios.opening_query: The housing authority terminated our rental assistance over paperwork we never received.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Welfare || Real Property > Section 8/HUD
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's our Section 8 voucher — the termination skipped the informal hearing HUD rules require, and our landlord starts eviction the day the subsidy stops.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's our Section 8 voucher — they skipped the required informal hearing, and eviction starts the day the subsidy stops.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether Section 8/HUD procedures govern the termination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the state's emergency rent-assistance program, not HUD — a state-agency appeal with its own deadline.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the state emergency rent program, not HUD — a state appeal with its own deadline.
scenarios.vetting_note: Program identity selects the HUD-specific label the runtime taxonomy provides.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm a nurse and a patient complaint from two years ago has suddenly come back to life.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Professional Licensing || General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's revived because the patient filed a malpractice suit naming me personally — the board matter closed long ago; the lawsuit is what needs defending.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's back because the patient sued — malpractice, naming me personally. The board matter closed long ago.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a civil malpractice suit rather than board action is live
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No lawsuit — the board reopened its own investigation with new 'evidence' and my license hearing is set.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No lawsuit — the board reopened its investigation and my hearing is set.
scenarios.vetting_note: Suit-vs-board revival of the same complaint.

scenarios.opening_query: The state overpaid me pandemic unemployment through their own error and now wants $23,000 with penalties.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I lost my waiver appeal — the debt is final, collection has begun, and with my other debts the realistic question is whether bankruptcy reaches an agency overpayment.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The waiver appeal is lost and collection's begun — the real question now is whether bankruptcy reaches this overpayment along with my other debts.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the agency debt is final leaving only debt-relief options
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The waiver window is still open — agency error qualifies, and the appeal deadline is in three weeks.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The waiver window's open — agency error qualifies, and I have three weeks.
scenarios.vetting_note: Appeal-exhausted posture converts agency practice into debt relief.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother's in-home care hours were slashed and the caseworker just says 'new assessment tool.'
scenarios.final: General Litigation > Americans With Disabilities Act
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid || General Litigation > Americans With Disabilities Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The cut forces her toward a nursing facility — advocates say institutionalizing people who can live at home with services violates the ADA's community-integration mandate, and there's a class case forming.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The cut pushes her into a facility — advocates say that violates the ADA's community-integration mandate, and a class case is forming.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether institutionalization-forcing cuts raise ADA claims
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She can manage at home either way — the fight is her individual fair-hearing appeal of the assessment math.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She can stay home either way — it's her individual fair-hearing appeal of the math.
scenarios.vetting_note: Olmstead-style facts elevate a benefits cut to disability rights.

scenarios.opening_query: The city denied my sidewalk café permit while approving everyone else's on the block.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal) || Business and Corporate > Restaurants/OLCC
scenarios.hidden_fact: The denial letter says it's because my OLCC sidewalk-service endorsement lapsed — fixing the liquor-license endorsement is the actual unlock.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The letter says my OLCC sidewalk-service endorsement lapsed — fixing that liquor endorsement is the unlock.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a liquor-license condition drives the permit denial
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No liquor angle — it's the planning office claiming my frontage is two inches too narrow, which their own map contradicts.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No liquor angle — planning claims my frontage is two inches short, and their own map says otherwise.
scenarios.vetting_note: OLCC-condition fact reroutes to the restaurant/liquor specialty.
